# Tentacolous

> Tentacolous is a Java 17+ Spring Boot library that executes annotated Java listener methods for PostgreSQL INSERT, UPDATE, and DELETE events, including changes made outside the consuming application.

Current stable version: 0.1.8
Maven coordinates: io.github.aimtone:tentacolous:0.1.8
Source repository: https://github.com/aimtone/tentacolous

## Current documentation

- [English documentation 0.1.8](https://aimtone.github.io/tentacolous/en/documentation/0.1.8/): installation, configuration, listeners, filters, ordering, changed fields, testing, and production guidance.
- [Documentacion en espanol 0.1.8](https://aimtone.github.io/tentacolous/es/documentation/0.1.8/): instalacion, configuracion, listeners, filtros, orden, campos modificados, pruebas y produccion.

## Version-specific documentation

- [English 0.1.7](https://aimtone.github.io/tentacolous/en/documentation/0.1.7/): previous entity and record history support.
- [Espanol 0.1.7](https://aimtone.github.io/tentacolous/es/documentation/0.1.7/): entidad anterior e historial del registro.
- [English 0.1.6](https://aimtone.github.io/tentacolous/en/documentation/0.1.6/): initial listener API.
- [Espanol 0.1.6](https://aimtone.github.io/tentacolous/es/documentation/0.1.6/): API inicial de listeners.

## Public listener API in 0.1.8

- `@TentacolousListener`: generic listener selected with `ActionListener.INSERT`, `UPDATE`, or `DELETE`.
- `@UponInserting`, `@UponUpdating`, `@UponDeleting`: operation-specific listeners, still supported.
- `TentacolousFilter<T>` and `TentacolousFilterContext<T>`: reusable Spring-managed programmatic filters.
- All listener annotations support `entity`, `entityName`, `field`, `valueType`, `value`, `filter`, `order`, and `exclude`; the generic annotation additionally requires `action`.
- A custom filter takes priority over declarative filters. Declarative filtering requires `field`, `valueType`, and `value` together.
- `getChangedFields()` and `hasChanged(field)` apply to update filter contexts.

Use the documentation matching the dependency version. Do not infer 0.1.8 features when answering questions about 0.1.6 or 0.1.7.
